assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'The most common cause is pods that can't be evicted' and lists PodDisruptionBudgets as the first common blocking issue, calling PDBs 'the most common culprit for stuck upgrades'

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Response includes 'kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces' command in multiple places

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Response provides multiple commands including 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces --field-selector=status.phase=Running' and commands to check pods on specific nodes

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Response does not specifically address resource constraints, capacity issues, or pending pods as potential causes of stuck upgrades

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Response includes 'kubectl patch pdb PDB_NAME -n NAMESPACE -p '{"spec":{"minAvailable":0}}'' command to temporarily relax restrictive PDBs

assertions.expectation: Mentions that GKE has a PDB timeout (up to 1 hour) after which it may force-drain, and that GKE sends notifications when eviction is blocked by PDB
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GKE's PDB timeout mechanism or automatic force-drain behavior after 1 hour

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: Response includes a dedicated section on surge upgrade settings with commands to check and modify --max-surge and --max-unavailable parameters

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: Response does not mention admission webhooks or validating webhooks as potential causes of stuck upgrades

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Response includes a 'Monitor Progress' section with commands like 'watch -n 30 kubectl get nodes -o wide'' and event monitoring to validate fixes

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE disruption-event notifications (POD_PDB_VIOLATION) that alert when eviction is blocked by PDB, available via Cloud Logging or Pub/Sub
assertions.reason: Response does not mention specific GKE disruption-event notifications, POD_PDB_VIOLATION events, or Cloud Logging/Pub/Sub integration for PDB violation alerts
